def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    # Rabin-Karp with rolling hash - optimized
    base = 256
    mod = (1 << 61) - 1  # Mersenne prime for faster modulo
    results = []
    # Precompute ord values
    text_ords = [ord(c) for c in text]
    pat_ords = [ord(c) for c in pattern]
    # Compute hash of pattern and first window
    pat_hash = 0
    txt_hash = 0
    h = pow(base, m - 1, mod)
    for i in range(m):
        pat_hash = (base * pat_hash + pat_ords[i]) % mod
        txt_hash = (base * txt_hash + text_ords[i]) % mod
    # Slide the pattern
    results_append = results.append
    for i in range(n - m + 1):
        if pat_hash == txt_hash:
            match = True
            for j in range(m):
                if text_ords[i + j] != pat_ords[j]:
                    match = False
                    break
            if match:
                results_append(i)
        if i < n - m:
            txt_hash = (base * (txt_hash - text_ords[i] * h) + text_ords[i + m]) % mod
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    # Rabin-Karp with double hashing to minimize false positives
    base1 = 256
    mod1 = (1 << 61) - 1
    base2 = 131
    mod2 = (1 << 31) - 1
    # Precompute ord values for speed
    text_ords = [ord(c) for c in text]
    pat_ords = [ord(c) for c in pattern]
    # Compute hashes of pattern and first window
    pat_h1 = pat_h2 = 0
    txt_h1 = txt_h2 = 0
    h1 = pow(base1, m - 1, mod1)
    h2 = pow(base2, m - 1, mod2)
    for i in range(m):
        pat_h1 = (base1 * pat_h1 + pat_ords[i]) % mod1
        pat_h2 = (base2 * pat_h2 + pat_ords[i]) % mod2
        txt_h1 = (base1 * txt_h1 + text_ords[i]) % mod1
        txt_h2 = (base2 * txt_h2 + text_ords[i]) % mod2
    results = []
    results_append = results.append
    for i in range(n - m + 1):
        if txt_h1 == pat_h1 and txt_h2 == pat_h2:
            # Double hash match - verify to be safe
            match = True
            for j in range(m):
                if text_ords[i + j] != pat_ords[j]:
                    match = False
                    break
            if match:
                results_append(i)
        if i < n - m:
            txt_h1 = (base1 * (txt_h1 - text_ords[i] * h1) + text_ords[i + m]) % mod1
            txt_h2 = (base2 * (txt_h2 - text_ords[i] * h2) + text_ords[i + m]) % mod2
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        # Single character optimization
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Rabin-Karp with double hashing
    base1 = 256
    mod1 = (1 << 61) - 1
    base2 = 131
    mod2 = (1 << 31) - 1
    text_ords = [ord(c) for c in text]
    pat_ords = [ord(c) for c in pattern]
    pat_h1 = pat_h2 = 0
    txt_h1 = txt_h2 = 0
    h1 = pow(base1, m - 1, mod1)
    h2 = pow(base2, m - 1, mod2)
    for i in range(m):
        pat_h1 = (base1 * pat_h1 + pat_ords[i]) % mod1
        pat_h2 = (base2 * pat_h2 + pat_ords[i]) % mod2
        txt_h1 = (base1 * txt_h1 + text_ords[i]) % mod1
        txt_h2 = (base2 * txt_h2 + text_ords[i]) % mod2
    results = []
    append = results.append
    nm = n - m
    for i in range(nm + 1):
        if txt_h1 == pat_h1 and txt_h2 == pat_h2:
            match = True
            for j in range(m):
                if text_ords[i + j] != pat_ords[j]:
                    match = False
                    break
            if match:
                append(i)
        if i < nm:
            ti = text_ords[i]
            tim = text_ords[i + m]
            txt_h1 = (base1 * (txt_h1 - ti * h1) + tim) % mod1
            txt_h2 = (base2 * (txt_h2 - ti * h2) + tim) % mod2
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Convert to bytes for faster indexing
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Rabin-Karp with single large prime (Mersenne)
    base = 256
    mod = (1 << 61) - 1
    pat_hash = 0
    txt_hash = 0
    h = pow(base, mb - 1, mod)
    for i in range(mb):
        pat_hash = (base * pat_hash + pb[i]) % mod
        txt_hash = (base * txt_hash + tb[i]) % mod
    results = []
    append = results.append
    nmb = nb - mb
    for i in range(nmb + 1):
        if txt_hash == pat_hash:
            # Verify match
            if tb[i:i + mb] == pb:
                append(i)
        if i < nmb:
            txt_hash = (base * (txt_hash - tb[i] * h) + tb[i + mb]) % mod
    return results

def find_all(text, pattern):
    n = len(text)
    m = len(pattern)
    if m == 0:
        return []
    if m > n:
        return []
    if m == 1:
        c = pattern[0]
        return [i for i in range(n) if text[i] == c]
    # Convert to bytes for fast byte-level comparison and indexing
    tb = text.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(text, str) else text
    pb = pattern.encode('latin-1') if isinstance(pattern, str) else pattern
    nb = len(tb)
    mb = len(pb)
    # Hybrid: Rabin-Karp hash + last-char pre-check
    base = 256
    mod = (1 << 61) - 1
    pat_hash = 0
    txt_hash = 0
    h = pow(base, mb - 1, mod)
    for i in range(mb):
        pat_hash = (base * pat_hash + pb[i]) % mod
        txt_hash = (base * txt_hash + tb[i]) % mod
    results = []
    append = results.append
    last_pat = pb[mb - 1]
    first_pat = pb[0]
    nmb = nb - mb
    for i in range(nmb + 1):
        if txt_hash == pat_hash and tb[i] == first_pat and tb[i + mb - 1] == last_pat:
            if tb[i:i + mb] == pb:
                append(i)
        if i < nmb:
            txt_hash = (base * (txt_hash - tb[i] * h) + tb[i + mb]) % mod
    return results
